args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer to receive the mask

args.name: mask
args.desc: The mask to add to the layer

gimp-image-flatten.blurb: Flatten all visible layers into a single layer. Discard all invisible layers.
gimp-image-flatten.help: This procedure combines the visible layers in a manner analogous to merging with the CLIP_TO_IMAGE merge type. Non-visible layers are discarded, and the resulting image is stripped of its alpha channel.

gimp-image-freeze-layers.help:
This procedure freezes the layer list of the image, suppressing any updates to the Layers dialog in response to changes to the image's layers. This can significantly improve performance while applying changes affecting the layer list.

Each call to 'gimp-image-freeze-layers' should be matched by a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-thaw-layers', undoing its effects.

gimp-image-get-active-layer.blurb: Returns the specified image's active layer.
gimp-image-get-active-layer.help: If there is an active layer, its ID will be returned, otherwise, -1. If a channel is currently active, then no layer will be. If a layer mask is active, then this will return the associated layer.

gimp-image-get-default-new-layer-mode.blurb: Get the default mode for newly created layers of this image.
gimp-image-get-default-new-layer-mode.help: Returns the default mode for newly created layers of this image.

gimp-image-get-layer-by-name.blurb: Find a layer with a given name in an image.
gimp-image-get-layer-by-name.help: This procedure returns the layer with the given name in the specified image.

args.name: name
args.desc: The name of the layer to find

gimp-image-get-layer-by-tattoo.blurb: Find a layer with a given tattoo in an image.
gimp-image-get-layer-by-tattoo.help: This procedure returns the layer with the given tattoo in the specified image.

args.name: tattoo
args.desc: The tattoo of the layer to find

gimp-image-get-layer-position.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.
gimp-image-get-layer-position.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-get-item-position' instead.

gimp-image-get-layers.blurb: Returns the list of layers contained in the specified image.
gimp-image-get-layers.help: This procedure returns the list of layers contained in the specified image. The order of layers is from topmost to bottommost.

gimp-image-insert-channel.blurb: Add the specified channel to the image.
gimp-image-insert-channel.help: This procedure adds the specified channel to the image at the given position. Since channel groups are not currently supported, the parent argument must always be 0. The position argument specifies the location of the channel inside the stack, starting from the top (0) and increasing. If the position is specified as -1, then the channel is inserted above the active channel.

gimp-image-insert-layer.blurb: Add the specified layer to the image.
gimp-image-insert-layer.help: This procedure adds the specified layer to the image at the given position. If the specified parent is a valid layer group (See 'gimp-item-is-group' and 'gimp-layer-group-new') then the layer is added inside the group. If the parent is 0, the layer is added inside the main stack, outside of any group. The position argument specifies the location of the layer inside the stack (or the group, if a valid parent was supplied), starting from the top (0) and increasing. If the position is specified as -1 and the parent is specified as 0, then the layer is inserted above the active layer, or inside the group if the active layer is a layer group. The layer type must be compatible with the image base type.

gimp-image-insert-vectors.blurb: Add the specified vectors to the image.
gimp-image-insert-vectors.help: This procedure adds the specified vectors to the image at the given position. Since vectors groups are not currently supported, the parent argument must always be 0. The position argument specifies the location of the vectors inside the stack, starting from the top (0) and increasing. If the position is specified as -1, then the vectors is inserted above the active vectors.

gimp-image-lower-layer.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.
gimp-image-lower-layer.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item' instead.

gimp-image-lower-layer-to-bottom.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom' instead.
gimp-image-lower-layer-to-bottom.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-lower-item-to-bottom' instead.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to lower to bottom

gimp-image-merge-down.blurb: Merge the layer passed and the first visible layer below.
gimp-image-merge-down.help: This procedure combines the passed layer and the first visible layer below it using the specified merge type. A merge type of EXPAND_AS_NECESSARY expands the final layer to encompass the areas of the visible layers. A merge type of CLIP_TO_IMAGE clips the final layer to the extents of the image. A merge type of CLIP_TO_BOTTOM_LAYER clips the final layer to the size of the bottommost layer.

args.name: merge-layer
args.desc: The layer to merge down from

args.name: merge-type
args.desc: The type of merge { EXPAND-AS-NECESSARY (0), CLIP-TO-IMAGE (1), CLIP-TO-BOTTOM-LAYER (2) }

gimp-image-merge-layer-group.blurb: Merge the passed layer group's layers into one normal layer.
gimp-image-merge-layer-group.help: This procedure combines the layers of the passed layer group into a single normal layer, replacing the group.

gimp-image-merge-visible-layers.blurb: Merge the visible image layers into one.
gimp-image-merge-visible-layers.help: This procedure combines the visible layers into a single layer using the specified merge type. A merge type of EXPAND_AS_NECESSARY expands the final layer to encompass the areas of the visible layers. A merge type of CLIP_TO_IMAGE clips the final layer to the extents of the image. A merge type of CLIP_TO_BOTTOM_LAYER clips the final layer to the size of the bottommost layer.

args.name: merge-type
args.desc: The type of merge { EXPAND-AS-NECESSARY (0), CLIP-TO-IMAGE (1), CLIP-TO-BOTTOM-LAYER (2) }

gimp-image-pick-correlate-layer.blurb: Find the layer visible at the specified coordinates.
gimp-image-pick-correlate-layer.help: This procedure finds the layer which is visible at the specified coordinates. Layers which do not qualify are those whose extents do not pass within the specified coordinates, or which are transparent at the specified coordinates. This procedure will return -1 if no layer is found.

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate for the pick

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate for the pick

gimp-image-raise-layer.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.
gimp-image-raise-layer.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item' instead.

gimp-image-raise-layer-to-top.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item-to-top' instead.
gimp-image-raise-layer-to-top.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-image-raise-item-to-top' instead.

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to raise to top

gimp-image-remove-layer.blurb: Remove the specified layer from the image.
gimp-image-remove-layer.help: This procedure removes the specified layer from the image. If the layer doesn't exist, an error is returned. If there are no layers left in the image, this call will fail. If this layer is the last layer remaining, the image will become empty and have no active layer.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer from which to remove mask

gimp-image-resize-to-layers.blurb: Resize the image to fit all layers.
gimp-image-resize-to-layers.help: This procedure resizes the image to the bounding box of all layers of the image. All channels within the image are resized to the new size; this includes the image selection mask. All layers within the image are repositioned to the new image area.

gimp-image-set-active-layer.blurb: Sets the specified image's active layer.
gimp-image-set-active-layer.help: If the layer exists, it is set as the active layer in the image. Any previous active layer or channel is set to inactive. An exception is a previously existing floating selection, in which case this procedure will return an execution error.

gimp-image-thaw-layers.help:
This procedure thaws the layer list of the image, re-enabling updates to the Layers dialog.

This procedure should match a corresponding call to 'gimp-image-freeze-layers'.

gimp-layer-add-alpha.blurb: Add an alpha channel to the layer if it doesn't already have one.
gimp-layer-add-alpha.help: This procedure adds an additional component to the specified layer if it does not already possess an alpha channel. An alpha channel makes it possible to clear and erase to transparency, instead of the background color. This transforms layers of type RGB to RGBA, GRAY to GRAYA, and INDEXED to INDEXEDA.

gimp-layer-add-mask.blurb: Add a layer mask to the specified layer.
gimp-layer-add-mask.help: This procedure adds a layer mask to the specified layer. Layer masks serve as an additional alpha channel for a layer. This procedure will fail if a number of prerequisites aren't met. The layer cannot already have a layer mask. The specified mask must exist and have the same dimensions as the layer. The layer must have been created for use with the specified image and the mask must have been created with the procedure 'gimp-layer-create-mask'.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer to receive the mask

args.name: mask
args.desc: The mask to add to the layer

gimp-layer-copy.help: This procedure copies the specified layer and returns the copy. The newly copied layer is for use within the original layer's image. It should not be subsequently added to any other image. The copied layer can optionally have an added alpha channel. This is useful if the background layer in an image is being copied and added to the same image.

args.name: add-alpha
args.desc: Add an alpha channel to the copied layer (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-create-mask.blurb: Create a layer mask for the specified layer.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer to which to add the mask

args.name: mask-type
args.desc: The type of mask { ADD-MASK-WHITE (0), ADD-MASK-BLACK (1), ADD-MASK-ALPHA (2), ADD-MASK-ALPHA-TRANSFER (3), ADD-MASK-SELECTION (4), ADD-MASK-COPY (5), ADD-MASK-CHANNEL (6) }

gimp-layer-delete.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.
gimp-layer-delete.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-delete' instead.

gimp-layer-flatten.blurb: Remove the alpha channel from the layer if it has one.
gimp-layer-flatten.help: This procedure removes the alpha channel from a layer, blending all (partially) transparent pixels in the layer against the background color. This transforms layers of type RGBA to RGB, GRAYA to GRAY, and INDEXEDA to INDEXED.

gimp-layer-from-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified mask's layer , or -1 if none exists.

args.name: mask
args.desc: Mask for which to return the layer

gimp-layer-get-apply-mask.blurb: Get the apply mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-apply-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's apply mask setting. If the value is TRUE, then the layer mask for this layer is currently being composited with the layer's alpha channel.

gimp-layer-get-blend-space.blurb: Get the blend space of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-blend-space.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's blend space.

gimp-layer-get-composite-mode.blurb: Get the composite mode of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-composite-mode.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's composite mode.

gimp-layer-get-composite-space.blurb: Get the composite space of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-composite-space.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's composite space.

gimp-layer-get-edit-mask.blurb: Get the edit mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-edit-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's edit mask setting. If the value is TRUE, then the layer mask for this layer is currently active, and not the layer.

gimp-layer-get-linked.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.
gimp-layer-get-linked.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-linked' instead.

gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha.blurb: Get the lock alpha channel setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's lock alpha channel setting.

gimp-layer-get-mask.blurb: Get the specified layer's mask if it exists.
gimp-layer-get-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's mask, or -1 if none exists.

gimp-layer-get-mode.blurb: Get the combination mode of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-mode.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's combination mode.

gimp-layer-get-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.
gimp-layer-get-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-name' instead.

gimp-layer-get-opacity.blurb: Get the opacity of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-opacity.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's opacity.

gimp-layer-get-preserve-trans.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha' instead.
gimp-layer-get-preserve-trans.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-lock-alpha' instead.

gimp-layer-get-show-mask.blurb: Get the show mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-get-show-mask.help: This procedure returns the specified layer's show mask setting. This controls whether the layer or its mask is visible. TRUE indicates that the mask should be visible. If the layer has no mask, then this function returns an error.

gimp-layer-get-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.
gimp-layer-get-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-tattoo' instead.

gimp-layer-get-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.
gimp-layer-get-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-get-visible' instead.

gimp-layer-group-new.help:
This procedure creates a new layer group. Attributes such as layer mode and opacity should be set with explicit procedure calls. Add the new layer group (which is a kind of layer) with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command.
Other procedures useful with layer groups: 'gimp-image-reorder-item', 'gimp-item-get-parent', 'gimp-item-get-children', 'gimp-item-is-group'.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the layer group

gimp-layer-is-floating-sel.blurb: Is the specified layer a floating selection?
gimp-layer-is-floating-sel.help: This procedure returns whether the layer is a floating selection. Floating selections are special cases of layers which are attached to a specific drawable.

gimp-layer-mask.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-mask' instead.
gimp-layer-mask.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-get-mask' instead.

gimp-layer-new.help: This procedure creates a new layer with the specified width, height, and type. Name, opacity, and mode are also supplied parameters. The new layer still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command. Other attributes such as layer mask modes, and offsets should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to which to add the layer

args.name: type
args.desc: The layer type { RGB-IMAGE (0), RGBA-IMAGE (1), GRAY-IMAGE (2), GRAYA-IMAGE (3), INDEXED-IMAGE (4), INDEXEDA-IMAGE (5) }

args.name: mode
args.desc: The layer combination mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

gimp-layer-new-from-drawable.blurb: Create a new layer by copying an existing drawable.
gimp-layer-new-from-drawable.help: This procedure creates a new layer as a copy of the specified drawable. The new layer still needs to be added to the image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command. Other attributes such as layer mask modes, and offsets should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The source drawable from where the new layer is copied

args.name: dest-image
args.desc: The destination image to which to add the layer

gimp-layer-new-from-visible.blurb: Create a new layer from what is visible in an image.
gimp-layer-new-from-visible.help: This procedure creates a new layer from what is visible in the given image. The new layer still needs to be added to the destination image, as this is not automatic. Add the new layer with the 'gimp-image-insert-layer' command. Other attributes such as layer mask modes, and offsets should be set with explicit procedure calls.

args.name: image
args.desc: The source image from where the content is copied

args.name: dest-image
args.desc: The destination image to which to add the layer

gimp-layer-remove-mask.blurb: Remove the specified layer mask from the layer.
gimp-layer-remove-mask.help: This procedure removes the specified layer mask from the layer. If the mask doesn't exist, an error is returned.

args.name: layer
args.desc: The layer from which to remove mask

gimp-layer-resize.blurb: Resize the layer to the specified extents.
gimp-layer-resize.help: This procedure resizes the layer so that its new width and height are equal to the supplied parameters. Offsets are also provided which describe the position of the previous layer's content. This operation only works if the layer has been added to an image.

args.name: offx
args.desc: x offset between upper left corner of old and new layers: (old - new)

args.name: offy
args.desc: y offset between upper left corner of old and new layers: (old - new)

gimp-layer-resize-to-image-size.blurb: Resize a layer to the image size.
gimp-layer-resize-to-image-size.help: This procedure resizes the layer so that it's new width and height are equal to the width and height of its image container.

gimp-layer-scale.blurb: Scale the layer using the default interpolation method.
gimp-layer-scale.help: This procedure scales the layer so that its new width and height are equal to the supplied parameters. The 'local-origin' parameter specifies whether to scale from the center of the layer, or from the image origin. This operation only works if the layer has been added to an image. The interpolation method used can be set with 'gimp-context-set-interpolation'.

args.name: local-origin
args.desc: Use a local origin (as opposed to the image origin) (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: local-origin
args.desc: Use a local origin (as opposed to the image origin) (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: interpolation
args.desc: Type of interpolation { INTERPOLATION-NONE (0), INTERPOLATION-LINEAR (1), INTERPOLATION-CUBIC (2), INTERPOLATION-NOHALO (3), INTERPOLATION-LOHALO (4) }

gimp-layer-set-apply-mask.blurb: Set the apply mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-apply-mask.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's apply mask setting. This controls whether the layer's mask is currently affecting the alpha channel. If there is no layer mask, this function will return an error.

args.name: apply-mask
args.desc: The new layer's apply mask setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-set-blend-space.blurb: Set the blend space of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-blend-space.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's blend space.

args.name: blend-space
args.desc: The new layer blend space { LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-AUTO (0), LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-RGB-LINEAR (1), LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-RGB-PERCEPTUAL (2), LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-LAB (3) }

gimp-layer-set-composite-mode.blurb: Set the composite mode of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-composite-mode.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's composite mode.

args.name: composite-mode
args.desc: The new layer composite mode { LAYER-COMPOSITE-AUTO (0), LAYER-COMPOSITE-UNION (1), LAYER-COMPOSITE-CLIP-TO-BACKDROP (2), LAYER-COMPOSITE-CLIP-TO-LAYER (3), LAYER-COMPOSITE-INTERSECTION (4) }

gimp-layer-set-composite-space.blurb: Set the composite space of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-composite-space.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's composite space.

args.name: composite-space
args.desc: The new layer composite space { LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-AUTO (0), LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-RGB-LINEAR (1), LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-RGB-PERCEPTUAL (2), LAYER-COLOR-SPACE-LAB (3) }

gimp-layer-set-edit-mask.blurb: Set the edit mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-edit-mask.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's edit mask setting. This controls whether the layer or it's mask is currently active for editing. If the specified layer has no layer mask, then this procedure will return an error.

args.name: edit-mask
args.desc: The new layer's edit mask setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-set-linked.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-linked' instead.
gimp-layer-set-linked.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-linked' instead.

args.name: linked
args.desc: The new item linked state (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-set-lock-alpha.blurb: Set the lock alpha channel setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-lock-alpha.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's lock alpha channel setting.

args.name: lock-alpha
args.desc: The new layer's lock alpha channel setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-set-mode.blurb: Set the combination mode of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-mode.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's combination mode.

args.name: mode
args.desc: The new layer combination mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

gimp-layer-set-name.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-name' instead.
gimp-layer-set-name.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-name' instead.

gimp-layer-set-offsets.help: This procedure sets the offsets for the specified layer. The offsets are relative to the image origin and can be any values. This operation is valid only on layers which have been added to an image.

gimp-layer-set-opacity.blurb: Set the opacity of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-opacity.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's opacity.

gimp-layer-set-preserve-trans.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-set-lock-alpha' instead.
gimp-layer-set-preserve-trans.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-layer-set-lock-alpha' instead.

args.name: lock-alpha
args.desc: The new layer's lock alpha channel setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-set-show-mask.blurb: Set the show mask setting of the specified layer.
gimp-layer-set-show-mask.help: This procedure sets the specified layer's show mask setting. This controls whether the layer or its mask is visible. TRUE indicates that the mask should be visible. If there is no layer mask, this function will return an error.

args.name: show-mask
args.desc: The new layer's show mask setting (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-set-tattoo.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.
gimp-layer-set-tattoo.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-tattoo' instead.

gimp-layer-set-visible.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.
gimp-layer-set-visible.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-item-set-visible' instead.

args.name: visible
args.desc: The new item visibility (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-layer-translate.blurb: Translate the layer by the specified offsets.
gimp-layer-translate.help:
This procedure translates the layer by the amounts specified in the x and y arguments. These can be negative, and are considered offsets from the current position. This command only works if the layer has been added to an image. All additional layers contained in the image which have the linked flag set to TRUE w ill also be translated by the specified offsets.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-item-transform-translate' instead.
